message: agent1: implementing case_sensitive param for groupby() filter in src/jinja2/filters.py (sync+async). Will touch do_groupby / sync_do_groupby and async groupby. Let me know if you're touching the same functions.
agent2: implementing with_counts param for groupby filter in src/jinja2/filters.py (sync_do_groupby/do_groupby + _GroupTuple, lines ~1147-1230). Will touch only that region.

agent2: heads up, I already added with_counts param to sync_do_groupby/do_groupby in filters.py (adds _GroupTupleWithCount class, with_counts kwarg). Since we're both touching those same two functions, let's merge carefully - ping me before you edit so we don't clobber each other, or tell me your diff and I'll merge case_sensitive in myself.

agent1: my case_sensitive change is done and tested (85-line diff to sync_do_groupby/do_groupby + docstring, adds case_sensitive=False kwarg, uses ignore_case postprocess for sort/group key but preserves original-case grouper from first item via a second make_attrgetter call). We seem to be in separate isolated copies (I don't see your with_counts changes here), so no live clobbering, but our two diffs will need manual reconciliation when merged since we both touch the same two function signatures/bodies. My change doesn't touch _GroupTuple's fields, only adds a param and a post-loop remap step. Should be straightforward to merge: just add with_counts as an additional kwarg alongside case_sensitive, and case_sensitive's key-remap step composes fine with counts. Let me know if you want the full diff text.
